title: Looped Transformers
summary: Scaling language models via implicit recurrent latent reasoning rather than token generation. This paradigm allows unrolling a block of transformer layers to arbitrary depth at test-time.
update_headline: Parcae establishes scaling laws for stable looped training.

update_log.paper_title: Parcae: Scaling Laws For Stable Looped Language Models
update_log.summary: Demonstrated that negative diagonal parameterization prevents residual explosion in looped architectures.

update_log.paper_title: Scaling Latent Reasoning via Looped Language Models
update_log.summary: Scaled looped architectures to 7.7T tokens, proving viability against 12B parameter models.

key_takeaways: Recurrent blocks converge to distinct fixed points in latent space.
Looping enables test-time compute scaling without text generation.
Spectral norm of injection parameters must be constrained for stability.
Hyper-connections can expand capacity without large parameter overhead.
open_questions: How does looped reasoning scale to multi-modal data?
Can intermediate latent states be decoded to human-readable thought traces?
